","type":"text/x-markdown"},"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/README.md":{"title":"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/README.md","text":"# external-llm-help

llm-help pages for third-party TiddlyWiki plugins — a host plugin that contributes browsable, context-scoped documentation under the `plugins/<plugin-slug>` path of the [llm-help](https://github.com/rimir-cc/tw-llm-help) browser.

## What it does

When the LLM (via llm-connect) calls `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph")`, it gets a tree of help pages describing the `flibbles/graph` plugin — concepts, widgets, filters, recipes, gotchas — without that documentation having to live inside the third-party plugin itself.

Each documented plugin lives in its own subfolder under `pages/`, so adding a new plugin is a self-contained change.

## Requires

- [`$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help`](https://github.com/rimir-cc/tw-llm-help) — the page-tree browser and `llm_help` MCP tool.

## Currently documented

- `flibbles/graph` — TW5-Graph (graph visualization toolkit).
","type":"text/x-markdown"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/changelog":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/changelog","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"|!Version |!Date |!Changes |
|0.1.0 |2026-04-13 |Initial release: page-tree scaffolding under `plugins/*` and full llm-help docs for `flibbles/graph` (overview, concepts, widgets, filters, templates, recipes, gotchas — 21 pages) |
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/architecture":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/architecture","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","rdt.hub-category":"integration","rdt.hub-order":"2","rdt.order":"2","rdt.plugin-key":"external-llm-help","rdt.section":"Architecture","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! No JavaScript

This plugin contains zero JS modules. It is a pure shadow-tiddler contribution to the `llm-help` page tree -- restart is never required when adding or editing pages.

!! Page contract

Each page tiddler has:

* `tags: $:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page`
* `llm-help-path: plugins/<slug>[/segment[/segment...]]`
* `llm-help-context: plugins/<slug>` (one key per documented plugin)
* `llm-help-description`: one-liner shown in parent listings
* `llm-help-sort` (optional): numeric, controls sibling order
* `type: text/vnd.tiddlywiki`

!! On-disk vs in-tree

Filesystem layout uses subfolders (`pages/<slug>/<segment>/<segment>.tid`) for human readability. The TW tiddler title also includes those slashes. The two are independently determined -- TW filesystem reads the explicit `title:` header, slashes are legal.

!! Context gating

The `llm-help-context` field uses hierarchical prefix matching (see [[llm-help functions|$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/functions]]). Activating context `plugins/tw5-graph` makes every page in that subtree visible. Activating only `plugins` would make every documented plugin visible at once.

The wiki's active context is read from `$:/temp/rimir/llm-help/active-context`; the LLM may pass an explicit context argument to override.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/dev-workflow":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/dev-workflow","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","rdt.hub-category":"integration","rdt.hub-order":"2","rdt.order":"3","rdt.plugin-key":"external-llm-help","rdt.section":"Dev workflow","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Adding pages for a new plugin

# Run the project skill: ``/tw-derive-llmhelp-from-plugin <plugin-id>``
# Confirm the proposed page tree when prompted.
# The skill reads TW gotcha memories, analyses the plugin source, writes the pages, bumps the version, and adds a changelog entry.
# Verify in the wiki: open Settings → LLM Help, select context `plugins/<slug>`, browse into `plugins/<slug>`.

!! Adding pages by hand

Create files under `pages/<slug>/`. Naming convention:

* `pages/<slug>/root.tid` -- title `$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/<slug>/root`, path `plugins/<slug>` (no trailing segment).
* `pages/<slug>/<segment>.tid` -- intermediate level.
* `pages/<slug>/<segment>/<sub>.tid` -- deeper levels.

Use `llm-help-context: plugins/<slug>` on every page so the whole subtree is opt-in.

!! Editing pages

Pages are wikitext, rendered at call time -- no restart needed. Save the `.tid` and the running wiki picks it up immediately via filesystem-watcher.

!! Validating

* Ensure each intermediate path has a parent page (otherwise the help browser shows empty parents).
* Ensure no two pages share the same `llm-help-path` unless intentional (multi-mounting).
* Cross-check every wikitext example against the project's TW gotcha memories before committing.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/examples":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/examples","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","rdt.hub-category":"integration","rdt.hub-order":"2","rdt.order":"4","rdt.plugin-key":"external-llm-help","rdt.section":"Examples","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Currently shipped

* `plugins/tw5-graph` -- documents `flibbles/graph` (graph visualization toolkit). 21 pages covering concepts, widgets, filters, templates, recipes, gotchas.

!! Sample LLM call sequence

```
llm_help("plugins")                              -- list documented plugins
llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph")                    -- root: what is the plugin, when to use it
llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts")           -- conceptual model
llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/edges")     -- specific concept
llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/embed-neighbors")  -- task-oriented recipe
```

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/faq":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/faq","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","rdt.hub-category":"integration","rdt.hub-order":"2","rdt.order":"5","rdt.plugin-key":"external-llm-help","rdt.section":"FAQ","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Why not put the pages directly into each third-party plugin?

We can't modify third-party plugins. Even for rimir's own plugins we sometimes prefer a separate host so a single skill can manage all external documentation in one place. Rimir plugins ''may'' ship their own llm-help pages -- the orga plugin already does.

!! What about overlap with `tw-gotchas`?

`tw-gotchas` covers ''general'' TiddlyWiki pitfalls. Plugin-specific gotchas live in `plugins/<slug>/gotchas` and link back to the relevant `tw-gotchas/*` page when the underlying issue is generic.

!! Will activating one plugin's context show another plugin's pages?

No. Each plugin's context is `plugins/<slug>` (e.g. `plugins/tw5-graph`). They're independent unless you activate the parent `plugins` key.

!! Does this need a server restart when pages change?

No. Pages are shadow tiddlers (wikitext) -- the running wiki picks up edits immediately via filesystem-watcher.

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/overview":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/overview","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","rdt.hub-category":"integration","rdt.hub-category-name":"Integration","rdt.hub-order":"2","rdt.order":"1","rdt.plugin-key":"external-llm-help","rdt.plugin-name":"external llm help","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"llm-help pages for third-party TiddlyWiki plugins","rdt.section":"Overview","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! What it does

`external-llm-help` is a host plugin: it does nothing on its own, it ships ''llm-help pages'' that document third-party TiddlyWiki plugins under the `plugins/<plugin-slug>` subtree of the `llm_help` browser. The LLM running in a tiddler can then learn how to use those plugins through the same self-discoverable navigation it uses for the wiki's own help.

!! Layout

```
pages/
  plugins.tid                  -- root of the plugins/ subtree
  tw5-graph/
    root.tid                   -- llm-help-path: plugins/tw5-graph
    overview.tid
    concepts.tid + concepts/*.tid
    widgets.tid + widgets/*.tid
    filters.tid
    templates.tid
    recipes.tid + recipes/*.tid
    gotchas.tid
  <next-plugin>/
    ...
```

Every page is tagged `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` and gated by `llm-help-context: plugins/<plugin-slug>`. Hierarchical prefix matching means activating the context for a plugin makes its full subtree visible.

!! Why a host plugin

* Third-party plugins can't ship rimir-specific llm-help pages.
* Authoring docs in one place keeps versioning, dependencies, and CI simple.
* A single subfolder per plugin makes contributions self-contained.

!! How to add a new plugin's pages

Use the project skill ``/tw-derive-llmhelp-from-plugin <plugin-id>`` -- it reads TW gotcha memories first, analyses the plugin source, proposes a tree, and writes the pages into this plugin.

!! Prerequisites

* [[$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help]]

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/reference":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/reference","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","rdt.hub-category":"integration","rdt.hub-order":"2","rdt.order":"6","rdt.plugin-key":"external-llm-help","rdt.section":"Reference","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page">
<$fill $name="body">

!! Documented plugins

|!Plugin |!Context key |!Pages |!Notes |
|`flibbles/graph` (TW5-Graph) |`plugins/tw5-graph` |21 |Includes cross-link to the `tw-gotchas/cascades` memory |

!! Tiddler titles

* Root: `$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/<slug>/root`
* Other pages: `$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/<slug>/<segment>[/<sub>...]`

!! Field schema (per page)

|!Field |!Type |!Required |!Notes |
|`tags` |string |yes |Must include `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` |
|`llm-help-path` |string |yes |Hierarchical with `/`. Space-separated for multi-mounting |
|`llm-help-context` |string |yes |`plugins/<slug>` -- one key per plugin |
|`llm-help-description` |string |yes |One line shown in parent listings |
|`llm-help-sort` |string (numeric) |no |Controls sibling order |
|`type` |string |yes |`text/vnd.tiddlywiki` |

</$fill>
</$transclude>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/documentation":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/documentation","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<%if [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]has[plugin-type]] %>
<$tiddler tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/overview">
<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/doc/overview"/>
</$tiddler>
<%else%>
To display this documentation, the plugin [[$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template]] needs to be installed.

See the [[readme|$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/readme]] tab for a basic overview.
<%endif%>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/icon":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/icon","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","type":"image/svg+xml","text":"<svg width="22pt" height="22pt" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="maroon" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">

<path d="M14 17h7M17.5 14v6"/>
</svg>
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/plugins":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/plugins","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins","llm-help-description":"Documentation for installed third-party (and selected rimir) TiddlyWiki plugins","llm-help-sort":"50","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Installed Plugins

This subtree documents the wiki's installed plugins for LLM consumption. Each plugin lives at `plugins/<plugin-slug>` and is gated by its own context key.

Available plugins:

<$list filter="[all[shadows+tiddlers]tag[$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page]get[llm-help-path]enlist-input[]removeprefix[plugins/]splitbefore[/]removesuffix[/]] +[!is[blank]unique[]sort[]]" emptyMessage="(none yet)">
* ``plugins/<$text text=<<currentTiddler>>/>`` -- llm_help("plugins/<$text text=<<currentTiddler>>/>")
</$list>

To browse a plugin's documentation, navigate into `plugins/<slug>` -- you'll get an overview, conceptual model, widget reference, recipes, and a gotchas page. Activating the context key `plugins/<slug>` makes the whole subtree visible.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts/edges":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts/edges","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/edges","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Field edges vs function edges — declaring edge types via $:/config/flibbles/graph/edges/*","llm-help-sort":"02","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Edges

The plugin has two ''preset edge types'':

!! Field edges

Tiddlers under `$:/config/flibbles/graph/edges/fields/<fieldname>` declare that a tiddler field carries edges. The field's value is parsed via a relink ''fieldtype'' (`list`, `title`, `filter`, ...) selected per field at `$:/config/flibbles/relink/fields/<fieldname>`.

Example: a field-edge for the standard `tags` field already ships, registered at `$:/config/flibbles/graph/edges/fields/tags`. The properties JSON in the body styles the resulting edges (color, arrows, etc.).

!! Function edges

Tiddlers under `$:/config/flibbles/graph/edges/functions/<name>` declare that a filter expression generates edges. The filter receives each node as `<currentTiddler>` and outputs the targets.

Example: a function-edge for `links` ships with `filter: [all[current]links[]]`. The body JSON styles those edges.

!! Per-graph selection

A graph's `edges.fields` and `edges.functions` filters narrow which preset edge types apply for that graph. Default: all of them.

!! Auto-rendering edges in a graph

`<$edges.typed>` reads both edge-type registries and emits one `<$edge>` per derived target. See `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/edges-typed")`.

!! Filter operator: `gettyped`

Reads a tiddler field via the configured field-type, returning the parsed edge values. Used both by `<$edges.typed>` and by the `<$nodes.neighbors>` macro:

```
[<aTiddler>gettyped[tags]]    -- list of tag titles
[<aTiddler>gettyped[my-refs]] -- parsed via $:/config/flibbles/relink/fields/my-refs
```

!! Adding a custom edge type

See `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/add-field-edge")` and `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/add-function-edge")`.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts/graphs":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts/graphs","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/graphs","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Named graph tiddlers ($:/graph/*), sidebar registration, template selection","llm-help-sort":"01","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Graphs

A ''named graph'' is a JSON tiddler whose title starts with `$:/graph/`. Its fields configure the graph; its `text` is the JSON map of node positions.

Common fields:

|!Field |!Meaning |
|`filter` |Filter expression yielding the nodes |
|`template` |Title of a `$:/tags/flibbles/graph/Template` tiddler. Default: first template tagged. The shipped templates are `$:/plugins/flibbles/graph/templates/standard-graph` and `.../live-graph` |
|`edges.fields` |Filter restricting which preset field-edges to use (default: all) |
|`edges.functions` |Filter restricting which preset function-edges to use (default: all) |
|`graph.graph` |JSON of graph-level properties applied via `<$properties $for=graph>` |
|`graph.nodes` |JSON of node-level properties applied via `<$properties $for=nodes>` |
|`graph.edges` |JSON of edge-level properties applied via `<$properties $for=edges>` |
|`popup` |`yes`/`no` -- enable hover popups |
|`popup.template` |Tiddler title used as popup body |
|`popup.ms`, `popup.width`, `popup.height` |Popup tuning |
|`neighbors.incoming`, `neighbors.outgoing` |`1`/`0` -- include incoming/outgoing neighbor nodes |
|`neighbors.whitelist` |Filter limiting which tiddlers may appear as neighbors. Default `[!is[system]]` |
|`neighbors.interedges` |`yes`/`no` -- draw edges between neighbors |

!! Sidebar registration

The sidebar shows the graph listed in `$:/config/flibbles/graph/sidebar` (single text field with one title) and any other `$:/graph/*` tiddler `insertafter` it. The shipped default points at `$:/graph/Default`.

!! Auto-render in story river

The plugin contributes a `ViewTemplateBodyFilter` so opening any tiddler whose title starts with `$:/graph/` renders the configured graph instead of the raw JSON. The cascade entry positions before the default; see `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/gotchas")` for the cascade-ordering caveat.

!! Creating a new graph

Use the ''Settings → Graph → Graphs → +'' button (which opens `$:/plugins/flibbles/graph/ui/Modals/NewGraph`) or simply create a tiddler at `$:/graph/<name>` with `type: application/json` and a `filter` field.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts/nodes":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts/nodes","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/nodes","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Nodes — node stacks for filter-based per-tiddler styling","llm-help-sort":"03","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Nodes

A ''node'' is one tiddler rendered via `<$node>` (raw) or `<$node.default>` (caption / color / icon picked from common fields).

!! Node stacks (filter-based styling)

Tiddlers under `$:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack/<name>` declare a ''stack entry'' -- a `filter` field plus a JSON body of node properties. For each rendered node, the macro `<$properties.stack>` walks the stack and merges properties from every entry whose filter matches the node.

Order matters: later entries override earlier ones. The order is taken from the list field of `$:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack` plus any other tiddlers with that prefix appended in title order.

Example shipped in the dev wiki:

```
title:  $:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack/orga
type:   application/json
filter: [rrt.type[orga]]
body:   {"color":"#ffffff"}
```

This colors every tiddler with `rrt.type=orga` white inside any graph that uses `<$properties.stack>` (which `properties.settings` does by default).

!! Node properties (any scope)

|!Property |!Meaning |
|`label` |Display string. Default: caption else title |
|`color` |Fill color |
|`image` |Icon tiddler title |
|`physics` |`yes`/`no` |
|`hover`, `blur`, `drag`, `free` |Action strings (run when event fires) |
|`navigation` |Set to `no` to disable double-click navigation |

Set them via `<$properties>` (graph-wide) or per-node via `<$node ...>` attributes.

!! Writable nodes

`<$nodes.writable $field=<somefield>>` makes nodes you can drag-add (drop a tiddler) and delete. New nodes are written into the configured field of a host tiddler. See `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-writable")`.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts/properties":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts/properties","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/properties","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"The <$properties> widget — three scopes (graph/nodes/edges) and their inheritance model","llm-help-sort":"04","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Properties

`<$properties>` is the central composition primitive. It sets default attribute values for everything inside its body. Three scopes:

|!`$for` value |!Targets |
|`graph` (default) |the graph itself |
|`nodes` |all `<$node>` widgets inside |
|`edges` |all `<$edge>` widgets inside |

Each `<$properties>` only sets values; nested properties inherit and can override. A node attribute set directly on `<$node>` always wins over `<$properties>`.

!! Common pattern

```
<$graph>
  <$properties $for=nodes color=lightgray>
    <$properties $for=nodes color=red $filter="[tag[Important]]">
      <$list filter="[tag[Page]]">
        <$node.default/>
      </$list>
    </$properties>
  </$properties>
</$graph>
```

Nodes tagged `Important` are red; the rest are lightgray.

!! Filter-scoped properties

`<$properties $filter="[...]" ...>` applies its values only to nodes/edges matching the filter. Tied to the `<currentTiddler>` of the surrounding `<$list>`.

!! Per-node-instance data via `$tiddler` + `$field`

`<$properties $tiddler=<<tid>> $field="graph.nodes" $for=nodes>` reads the JSON object from `<<tid>>!!graph.nodes` and applies it. This is how a graph tiddler's `graph.nodes` field becomes the node defaults.

!! High-level wrapper macros

* `<$properties.persistent>` — record dragged positions to a data tiddler.
* `<$properties.popup>` — hover popup.
* `<$properties.stack>` — apply node-stack overrides.
* `<$properties.river>` — focus/zoom in story river vs sidebar.
* `<$properties.settings>` — combines popup + river + stack + reads the graph tiddler's own `graph.{graph,nodes,edges}` fields.

See `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/properties")` for parameter details.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/concepts","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/concepts","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Conceptual model — graphs, edges, nodes, properties","llm-help-sort":"03","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Concepts

The plugin's data model has four top-level concepts. Read them in this order before writing any graph wikitext:

* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/graphs")` — named graphs at `$:/graph/*`, sidebar registration, templates
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/edges")` — field edges vs function edges, configuration tiddlers
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/nodes")` — node stacks for per-tiddler styling
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/properties")` — the `<$properties>` widget and the three property scopes (graph / nodes / edges)
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/filters":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/filters","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/filters","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Custom filter operator (gettyped) and run prefix (:cache)","llm-help-sort":"05","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Filters

The plugin contributes:

!! Operator: `gettyped[<field>]`

Reads a tiddler field through its configured relink ''fieldtype'' (`list`, `title`, `filter`, ...). The fieldtype lookup is `$:/config/flibbles/relink/fields/<field>`; if absent, defaults to the `list` fieldtype.

```
[<src>gettyped[tags]]       -- titles from the tags field (list type)
[<src>gettyped[my-link]]    -- the single title in my-link (title type)
[<src>gettyped[my-filter]]  -- the tiddlers matched by the filter in my-filter (filter type)
```

Used by `<$edges.typed>` and `<$nodes.neighbors>`. You'd normally invoke it manually only when building custom edge generators.

!! Run prefix: `:cache[...]`

Caches the result of a filter run keyed on the input. Subsequent calls with the same input skip evaluation. Used heavily inside `<$nodes.neighbors>` and `<$properties.stack>` to keep large graphs responsive.

```
:cache[all[shadows+tiddlers]subfilter<myExpensiveFilter>]
```

Caveats:

* Cache invalidates when the dependency set changes (additions, deletions, edits to relevant fields). It does NOT detect changes inside variables passed in as the input -- if you pass `<$let myFilter=...>`, vary the value to vary the cache key.
* Don't use `:cache` for tiny filters; the overhead loses to plain evaluation.

!! Common companions (built-in TW)

`<$edges.typed>` etc. lean heavily on `:map:flat`, `subfilter`, and `gettyped`. If you write custom edge generators, also see the project's TW gotchas, especially:

* `:map` returns only the first result per item (`tw-gotchas/filter-runs`).
* `:filter` does not support `!` negation.
* `subfilter` cannot embed a literal filter expression -- pass it via `<variable>` or `{tiddler}`.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/gotchas":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/gotchas","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/gotchas","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Known pitfalls when working with TW5-Graph","llm-help-sort":"08","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Gotchas

!! 1. Cascade ordering — graph's ViewTemplateBodyFilter can break later cascade entries

In TiddlyWiki 5.3.8, `flibbles/graph` has been observed to break the `ViewTemplateBodyFilter` cascade chain when a third-party cascade entry runs after it.

Symptom: a custom cascade filter works alone but returns empty when the full cascade runs.

Fix: position custom cascade filters early, before graph's entry. Use `list-before: $:/config/ViewTemplateBodyFilters/testcase` (first-in-list) rather than appending late.

Cross-reference: `llm_help("tw-gotchas/cascades")` covers this in detail.

!! 2. Engine plugin must be installed

Without an engine plugin (`flibbles/vis-network` or alternative), `<$graph>` parses but renders nothing useful. Check `$:/config/flibbles/graph/engine` and Settings → Graph → Engine.

!! 3. `gettyped` requires a registered fieldtype for non-default fields

If you define a custom field for edges and don't add `$:/config/flibbles/relink/fields/<field>`, `gettyped` falls back to the default `list` fieldtype. For single-title fields this silently mis-parses. Always register the fieldtype together with the edge preset.

!! 4. Position persistence requires a writable graph tiddler

`<$properties.persistent>` writes positions back to a JSON tiddler. If the host tiddler is read-only (e.g. shadow from a plugin), positions reset on every render. The standard template handles this automatically by tagging the data tiddler `$:/tags/flibbles/graph/TiddlerData`.

!! 5. `:cache` invalidation around variable inputs

The plugin uses `:cache` heavily inside `<$nodes.neighbors>` and `<$properties.stack>`. Cache keys derive from the input values to the run prefix, not from the variables in scope. If you compose your own caching with `:cache[subfilter<myFilter>]`, varying `<myFilter>` actually varies the input — but vary the input only when needed.

!! 6. `$properties` `$tiddler`/`$field` JSON shape

When you read properties from `$tiddler`+`$field`, the field must be a JSON object whose keys are the property names. A bare string is silently ignored. Bad shape: `"color": "red"` as the top-level body. Good: `{"color":"red"}`.

!! 7. Subfilter cache tiddler is global

`$:/plugins/flibbles/graph/subfilters` is consulted by every `<$edges.typed>` and `<$nodes.neighbors>`. Don't shadow it from your own plugin — override only the specific subfilter index you need.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/overview":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/overview","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/overview","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"What TW5-Graph does, its engine model, and prerequisites","llm-help-sort":"02","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Overview

!! What it does

Provides widgets to declare ''nodes'' and ''edges'' from tiddler data, plus a render container (`<$graph>`) that hands them to a visualization engine. The engine is a separate plugin that registers itself via `module-type: graphengine` (e.g. `flibbles/vis-network`).

!! Engine model

```
flibbles/graph              -- the toolkit (widgets, presets, settings UI)
flibbles/vis-network        -- a graph engine (DOM rendering, layout)
```

The active engine is selected in Control Panel → Settings → Graph → Engine and stored in `$:/config/flibbles/graph/engine`. Without an engine installed, the widgets parse but render nothing meaningful.

!! Source of nodes and edges

* ''Nodes'' come from a filter (you decide what tiddlers become nodes).
* ''Edges'' come from one of two ''preset edge types'':
** ''Field edges'': edges derived from a tiddler field (e.g. `tags`, `list`, or any custom field whose values are titles or filters).
** ''Function edges'': edges derived from a filter expression evaluated per node (e.g. `links`, `transcludes`).

Both are configured under `$:/config/flibbles/graph/edges/{fields,functions}/<name>`.

!! Persistence

Node positions and per-graph settings are stored in the graph's defining tiddler (or, for ad-hoc graphs, in a JSON data tiddler tagged `$:/tags/flibbles/graph/TiddlerData`). The `<$properties.persistent>` macro handles writing positions back.

!! Prerequisites

* TiddlyWiki ≥ 5.3.0
* A graph engine plugin (`flibbles/vis-network` or equivalent)

!! Project home

https://flibbles.github.io/tw5-graph
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/add-field-edge":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/add-field-edge","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/add-field-edge","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Define a new field-edge type so a custom field becomes graph edges","llm-help-sort":"02","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Recipe: add a custom field-edge type

Goal: a tiddler has a field `assignees` (list of titles). You want each title in `assignees` to become a graph edge.

!! Step 1 — register the relink fieldtype

Tiddler `$:/config/flibbles/relink/fields/assignees`:

```
title: $:/config/flibbles/relink/fields/assignees
text:  list
```

(Pick `list`, `title`, or `filter` to match how you store the field.)

!! Step 2 — register the field as a graph edge type

Tiddler `$:/config/flibbles/graph/edges/fields/assignees` (`type: application/json`):

```json
{
  "color": "#3f8efc",
  "arrows": "to",
  "label": "assigned"
}
```

(JSON properties supported depend on the engine; for `vis-network`, the keys map directly to vis edge options.)

!! Step 3 — graphs pick it up automatically

Any graph using `<$edges.typed>` (default in both shipped templates) will now draw `assignees` edges. To exclude this preset for a specific graph, set its `edges.fields` field to a filter that excludes `assignees`, e.g. `[all[]] -[[assignees]]`.

!! Verification

In the wiki:

* Open Settings → Graph → Properties tab → Edges → Fields. Your new type should appear.
* Open `$:/graph/Default` (or any graph) and confirm the new edges render.

!! Notes

* The presets list is cached in `$:/plugins/flibbles/graph/subfilters` -- changes are picked up automatically; no restart needed.
* If your field is a single title (not a list), use `title` as the relink fieldtype. The plugin will then read it via `gettyped` correctly.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/add-function-edge":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/add-function-edge","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/add-function-edge","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Define a new function-edge type so a filter becomes graph edges","llm-help-sort":"03","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Recipe: add a custom function-edge type

Goal: draw an edge from each project tiddler to the people listed in its `lead` field but only for projects tagged `Active`.

A function-edge is a filter that, given a node title via `<currentTiddler>`, outputs target titles.

!! Definition

Tiddler `$:/config/flibbles/graph/edges/functions/active-leads` (`type: application/json`):

```json
{
  "color": "#cf0",
  "arrows": "to",
  "label": "leads"
}
```

Plus a `filter` field on the same tiddler:

```
filter: [tag[Active]gettyped[lead]]
```

The filter receives one node at a time as `<currentTiddler>` and returns the edge targets. Here it short-circuits to nothing for non-Active tiddlers (because `tag[Active]` filters them out before the `gettyped` runs).

!! Pickup

Same as field-edges -- the preset list is cached in `$:/plugins/flibbles/graph/subfilters` and refreshes automatically. Any graph using `<$edges.typed>` will now show the edges.

!! Filter authoring tips

* The filter input is a single title. Use `<currentTiddler>` references explicitly if you need the input — e.g. `[<currentTiddler>links[]]` is what the shipped `links` preset does.
* Don't `:cache` the filter itself — `<$edges.typed>` already caches the preset list. Caching inside the per-node filter would key on a single title and waste memory.
* Avoid `:filter` with negation. See the project's `tw-gotchas/filters` page.

!! Per-graph selection

To use this preset only on selected graphs, set the graph's `edges.functions` field, e.g. `[[active-leads]] [[links]]` to use only those two function presets.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/embed-neighbors":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/embed-neighbors","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/embed-neighbors","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Embed an inline graph of the current tiddler + its neighbors","llm-help-sort":"01","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Recipe: embed an inline neighbor graph

Goal: drop a graph into a tiddler's body that shows the tiddler itself plus everything connected to it via field-edges and link-edges.

!! Solution

Add to the tiddler's body:

```
<$graph>
  <$properties $for=nodes color="#cccccc" />

  <!-- the anchor node -->
  <$node.default/>

  <!-- everything connected to it -->
  <$nodes.neighbors
    $filter="[<currentTiddler>]"
    $fields="[all[]]"
    $functions="[all[]]"
    $interedges=yes />

  <!-- edges from the anchor to its neighbors -->
  <$edges.typed/>
</$graph>
```

!! Variations

* Limit which neighbors appear: `$whitelist="[!is[system]!is[shadow]]"`.
* Restrict edge types: `$fields="[[tags]]"` `$functions="[[links]]"` (use a literal-list filter to allow only the named presets).
* Disable inter-neighbor edges: omit `$interedges` (or set to `no`).
* For a richer interactive experience, wrap in `<$properties.settings>` so popups, the node stack and graph-tiddler-stored properties also apply -- but you need a graph tiddler for that. Cleaner approach for a one-off embed: stick with raw `<$graph>` as above.

!! Why not the live-graph template?

You ''can'' point a `$:/graph/...` tiddler at the current tiddler via `filter={{!!title}}` and transclude it -- but that requires a separate graph tiddler. The inline form above is fully self-contained and re-renders when the current tiddler's edges change.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/hover-popups":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/hover-popups","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/hover-popups","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Configure hover popups for nodes — template, size, delay","llm-help-sort":"05","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Recipe: hover popups

Goal: hovering a node shows a small floating preview rendered from a template.

!! Per-graph configuration (named graphs)

On the graph tiddler `$:/graph/<name>`, set fields:

|!Field |!Value |
|`popup` |`yes` |
|`popup.template` |Title of a tiddler whose body is rendered as the popup. The current node title is bound to `<currentTiddler>` |
|`popup.ms` |Hover delay in ms before the popup opens. Default `600` |
|`popup.width` |CSS width. Default `300px` |
|`popup.height` |CSS height. Default `200px` |

The shipped Edit template tab "Popups" gives you a UI for these.

!! Inline graph

When you build your own template, wrap content in `<$properties.popup>`:

```
<$properties.popup
  $enabled=yes
  $ms=400
  $width="320px"
  $height="240px"
  $for=nodes >
  <!-- nested $node / $list etc. -->
</$properties.popup>
```

!! Popup body

Inside the popup template tiddler, `<currentTiddler>` is the hovered node. A simple template:

```
!!! <$link><$view field=title/></$link>

<$view field=description/>
```

For something more visual you can transclude the tiddler itself:

```
<$transclude $tiddler=<<currentTiddler>> $mode=block />
```

!! Disable

Set `popup` to `no` (or just leave the field absent — default is no popup).
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/style-by-type":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes/style-by-type","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/style-by-type","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Color/style nodes per tiddler type using a node stack","llm-help-sort":"04","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Recipe: style nodes by tiddler type

Goal: in any graph, projects are blue, tasks are orange, people are gray.

!! Define stack entries

Three tiddlers, each tagged with `type: application/json`:

`$:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack/projects`:

```
filter: [rrt.type[project]]
text:   {"color":"#3f8efc"}
```

`$:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack/tasks`:

```
filter: [rrt.type[task]]
text:   {"color":"#f59e0b"}
```

`$:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack/people`:

```
filter: [rrt.type[person]]
text:   {"color":"#9ca3af"}
```

Each stack entry has a `filter` field deciding which nodes it applies to and a JSON body holding the property overrides.

!! Pickup

`<$properties.stack>` walks the stack automatically. Both shipped templates wrap their content in `<$properties.settings>`, which calls `<$properties.stack>`. So new entries take effect without restart and without per-graph configuration.

!! Order

Order is taken from the list field of the parent `$:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack` (auto-discovered tiddlers are appended in title-sort order). Later entries override earlier ones — useful if a node matches multiple filters.

!! Local override per graph

`<$properties $for=nodes $filter="[some-filter]" color=red>` directly inside a graph wins over stack entries (set on a more specific scope).
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/recipes","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/recipes","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Task-oriented recipes for common in-tiddler graph workflows","llm-help-sort":"07","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Recipes

Common tasks the user might want to do "in the context of a tiddler":

* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/embed-neighbors")` — show an inline graph of the current tiddler and its neighbors
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/add-field-edge")` — define a custom edge type from a tiddler field
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/add-function-edge")` — define a custom edge type from a filter
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/style-by-type")` — color nodes per tiddler type via a node stack
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes/hover-popups")` — configure popup template / size / delay
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/root":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/root","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"TW5-Graph (flibbles/graph) — toolkit of widgets for graph visualization in TiddlyWiki","llm-help-sort":"01","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"TW5-Graph

`flibbles/graph` is a toolkit of widgets that turns tiddlers into nodes of an interactive graph. It does NOT ship a visualization library — it requires a separate engine plugin (most commonly `flibbles/vis-network`).

Two ways to use it:

# As a ''sidebar / modal graph'' — JSON tiddlers under `$:/graph/<name>` define named graphs registered in the sidebar via `$:/config/flibbles/graph/sidebar`.
# As an ''inline graph in a tiddler body'' — render `<$graph>...</$graph>` directly in wikitext (typically with the `live-graph` template).

The ''ViewTemplateBodyFilter'' shipped by the plugin auto-renders any tiddler whose title starts with `$:/graph/` as the configured graph. Tiddlers whose title starts with `$:/config/flibbles/graph/<type>/<category>` get an editor UI for the corresponding preset.

Navigate deeper:

* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/overview")` — what it does, engine model, prerequisites
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts")` — graphs, edges, nodes, properties
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets")` — widget & macro reference
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/filters")` — `gettyped`, `:cache`
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/templates")` — standard vs live graph
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/recipes")` — common in-tiddler tasks
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/gotchas")` — known pitfalls
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/templates":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/templates","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/templates","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"standard-graph vs live-graph — when to pick which template","llm-help-sort":"06","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Templates

Two shipped templates, both tagged `$:/tags/flibbles/graph/Template`. A graph tiddler's `template` field selects one; otherwise the first-tagged template wins.

!! `$:/plugins/flibbles/graph/templates/standard-graph`

The interactive editor template:

* Persistent positions (via `<$properties.persistent>` writing back into the graph tiddler).
* Settings popup wired in (click the gear-icon node).
* `<$nodes.writable $field=filter>` -- you can drop / add / delete nodes; the changes write back into the graph tiddler's `filter` field.
* Pulls everything together via `<$properties.settings>`.

Use this for ''interactive editing'' graphs.

!! `$:/plugins/flibbles/graph/templates/live-graph`

Filter-driven, no persistence:

* Reads the graph tiddler's `filter` field.
* Re-renders fully when the filter result changes (the template uses a `focusTiddler` set to force fresh rendering).
* No drag-position persistence -- positions are layout-engine-driven.
* Ideal for ''embedding a graph inside another tiddler's body'' where the node set should track live data.

Use this for ''dashboards'' and ''inline tiddler embeds''.

!! Custom templates

Tag a tiddler `$:/tags/flibbles/graph/Template`. Inside the body, render `<$graph>` and use the `<$properties.*>` family. Use `<$properties.settings>` if you want the standard popup/river/stack pipeline; otherwise compose only what you need.

!! Picking a template at view time

`<$graph.view $tiddler=<<myGraph>> $mode=block>` chooses the template via the graph's `template` field. Pass `$$template=<<otherTemplate>>` (note the double-`$`) to override per call.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/edge":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/edge","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/edge","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"<$edge> primitive and the $action.addedge / $action-addtyped helpers","llm-help-sort":"03","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"`<$edge>`, `$action.addedge`, `$action-addtyped`

!! `<$edge>`

Declares one edge. Required attributes:

|!Attribute |!Meaning |
|`$id` |Stable unique id for the edge |
|`$from` |Source node title (default: `<currentTiddler>`) |
|`$to` |Target node title (default: the surrounding `<toTiddler>` from `<$edges.typed>`'s `<$properties $for=edges>` context) |

Use `<$edge>` directly only when you're hand-rolling an edge generator. For automatic edges, prefer `<$edges.typed>` (`llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/edges-typed")`).

!! `$action.addedge`

A widget-macro that opens the ''SelectField'' modal so the user picks which preset field-edge to use, then runs `$action-addtyped`:

```
<$button>
  Add edge
  <$action.addedge/>
</$button>
```

It reads `<fromTiddler>` and `<toTiddler>` from scope (or its `$$fromTiddler` / `$$toTiddler` parameters).

!! `$action-addtyped` and `$action-removetyped`

Action widgets that mutate a tiddler field through the edge fieldtype:

```
<$action-addtyped    $tiddler=<<src>> $field=tags $value=<<dst>> />
<$action-removetyped $tiddler=<<src>> $field=tags $value=<<dst>> />
```

These respect the field's relink fieldtype, so `list`, `title`, `filter`, etc. all serialize correctly.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/edges-typed":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/edges-typed","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/edges-typed","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"<$edges.typed> — auto-generate edges from the configured preset edge types","llm-help-sort":"04","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"`<$edges.typed>`

Reads the registered preset edge types (`$:/config/flibbles/graph/edges/{fields,functions}/<name>`) and emits one `<$edge>` per derived target.

```
<$list filter="[tag[Page]]">
  <$node.default/>
  <$edges.typed/>
</$list>
```

Parameters:

|!Param |!Default |!Meaning |
|`$editable` |`no` |If `yes`, edges get a `delete` action that runs `$action-removetyped` |
|`$functions` |`[all[]]` |Filter narrowing which function-edge presets to use |
|`$fields` |`[all[]]` |Filter narrowing which field-edge presets to use |
|`$tiddler` |`<currentTiddler>` |Source node identity |

The widget pulls the active preset list from the cached `$:/plugins/flibbles/graph/subfilters` data tiddler so you don't pay the discovery cost per node.

!! Per-graph configuration

A graph's `edges.fields` and `edges.functions` fields (set via the graph's edit template or directly on the JSON tiddler) are passed through as `$fields` / `$functions` -- this is how `$:/graph/MyGraph` selects only certain edge presets.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/graph":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/graph","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/graph","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"<$graph> — the canvas container","llm-help-sort":"01","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"`<$graph>`

The render container. Always wrap node/edge declarations in a `<$graph>`.

```
<$graph>
  <$list filter="[tag[Page]]">
    <$node.default/>
  </$list>
</$graph>
```

It uses whichever engine is selected in `$:/config/flibbles/graph/engine`. Without an engine plugin installed it renders nothing.

Common attributes (most are normally set via `<$properties $for=graph>`, not the widget directly):

|!Attribute |!Meaning |
|`zoom` |`yes`/`no` — enable scroll-zoom |
|`navigation` |`no` to disable double-click navigation |
|`physics` |`yes`/`no` — engine-dependent layout simulation |

The `<$graph.view>` macro (note the dot-prefixed `$` meaning a widget-style macro) is a higher-level wrapper that loads a named graph by tiddler title and template:

```
<<$graph.view "$:/graph/Default">>
```

This is what the sidebar uses internally.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/node":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/node","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/node","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"<$node> and <$node.default>","llm-help-sort":"02","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"`<$node>` and `<$node.default>`

`<$node>` declares one node in the graph. It's typically used inside a `<$list>` so the surrounding `<currentTiddler>` is the node's source tiddler.

```
<$list filter="[tag[Page]]">
  <$node label={{!!caption}} color={{!!color}} image={{!!icon}} />
</$list>
```

Attributes (all optional; defaults inherit from the surrounding `<$properties $for=nodes>`):

|!Attribute |!Meaning |
|`$tiddler` |Override the node's identity (default: `<currentTiddler>`) |
|`$pos` |Pre-set position string `x,y` (used by `<$properties.persistent>`) |
|`label` |Display label |
|`color` |Fill color |
|`image` |Icon tiddler title |
|`physics` |`yes`/`no` |

!! `<$node.default>`

Convenience macro that wikifies the caption (or title) into the label and reads `color`/`icon` fields:

```
<$node.default/>
```

Equivalent to:

```
<$wikify name=label mode=inline text={{{ [{!!title}get[caption]else[<$text text={{!!title}}/>]] }}}>
  <$node $pos=<<pos>> label=<<label>> color={{!!color}} image={{!!icon}} />
</$wikify>
```

Use `<$node.default>` 95% of the time; reach for raw `<$node>` only when you need a custom label-rendering pipeline.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-neighbors":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-neighbors","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-neighbors","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"<$nodes.neighbors> — auto-render incoming + outgoing neighbors of a base set","llm-help-sort":"06","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"`<$nodes.neighbors>`

Given a base filter (the "anchor" tiddlers), renders all tiddlers that have an edge to or from the anchors -- both incoming and outgoing -- as nodes. Perfect for "show me everything connected to this tiddler".

```
<$nodes.neighbors
  $filter="[<currentTiddler>]"
  $fields="[all[]]"
  $functions="[all[]]"
  $interedges=yes
  $whitelist="[!is[system]]" />
```

Parameters:

|!Param |!Default |!Meaning |
|`$filter` |required |Filter producing the anchor nodes |
|`$fields` |`[all[]]` |Field-edge presets to use for neighbor discovery |
|`$functions` |`[all[]]` |Function-edge presets to use |
|`$interedges` |unset |If `yes`, also draw edges between two neighbors (not just anchor↔neighbor) |
|`$whitelist` |`[!is[system]]` |Filter limiting which tiddlers may appear as neighbors |

Anchors themselves should be rendered separately (with `<$node.default>`) before or after this widget; `<$nodes.neighbors>` only emits the surrounding nodes.

!! Performance

Heavy use of `:cache` internally (subfilters, regexp build, neighbor expansion). The widget computes `:cache[subfilter<incoming.raw>]` etc. once per render -- editing unrelated tiddlers will not re-evaluate the whole neighbor graph.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-writable":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-writable","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-writable","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"<$nodes.writable> — interactively add/remove nodes that persist to a tiddler field","llm-help-sort":"05","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"`<$nodes.writable>`

Renders nodes from a tiddler field and wires up double-click / drop / addEdge / delete actions so the graph is editable. The set of node tiddlers is read via `gettyped<field>`, so the same field-type rules used by edges apply.

```
<$nodes.writable $tiddler=<<myGraph>> $field=members>
  <!-- inside: $node.default + edges.typed etc. -->
</$nodes.writable>
```

Parameters:

|!Param |!Default |!Meaning |
|`$tiddler` |`{!!title}` |Tiddler whose field stores the node list |
|`$field` |required |Field name (e.g. `members`, `nodes`, custom) |
|`$variable` |`currentTiddler` |Variable to bind each node title into |

Behavior wired in:

* ''doubleclick'' / ''addNode'' — opens `SelectTiddler` modal; on confirm, creates a tiddler if missing, appends it via `$action-addtyped`, and records its position.
* ''drop'' — drag-drops a tiddler into the canvas → `$action-addtyped` to the configured field.
* ''addEdge'' — runs `$action.addedge` against the from/to nodes.
* ''delete'' — `$action-removetyped` for the dropped node.

The resulting node list survives reloads because positions are persisted via the surrounding `<$properties.persistent>` (the `standard-graph` template wires this up).
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/properties":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets/properties","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/properties","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"<$properties> and the .persistent/.popup/.stack/.river/.settings family","llm-help-sort":"07","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"`<$properties>` and friends

!! `<$properties>` (raw)

Sets default attribute values for nested nodes/edges/graphs. See `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/properties")` for the model.

Common attributes:

|!Attr |!Meaning |
|`$for` |`graph` (default), `nodes`, or `edges` -- which scope to target |
|`$filter` |Restrict to nodes/edges matching this filter (per `<currentTiddler>`) |
|`$tiddler`, `$field` |Read property values from a JSON field of a tiddler (per-instance properties) |
|any other |Becomes the default value for the targeted scope |

!! `<$properties.persistent $dataTiddler=<<tid>>>`

Wires up ''free'' (drag end) and ''delete'' actions so node positions are written to indices of `<<tid>>` and tags it `$:/tags/flibbles/graph/TiddlerData` if it isn't already (so the data survives wiki rebuilds).

!! `<$properties.popup $enabled $ms $width $height $for>`

Hover popup for nodes/edges/graphs. Reads `popup`, `popup.template`, `popup.ms`, `popup.width`, `popup.height` from the graph tiddler when used through `properties.settings`.

!! `<$properties.stack>`

Walks `[list[$:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack]]` and any other `$:/config/flibbles/graph/nodes/stack/*` tiddlers, applying each entry's properties to nodes that match its `filter`. See `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/concepts/nodes")`.

!! `<$properties.river $state>`

Tweaks zoom/focus/navigation behaviour for graphs rendered in the story river vs the sidebar. The sidebar gets `zoom=yes` automatically; the story-river version gates zoom on a focus state.

!! `<$properties.settings>`

Top-level convenience that combines popup, river, stack, and reads the graph tiddler's `graph.graph`/`graph.nodes`/`graph.edges` JSON fields. The `standard-graph` template uses this. If you build your own template, this is usually the right wrapper.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/pages/tw5-graph/widgets","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"plugins/tw5-graph/widgets","llm-help-context":"plugins/tw5-graph","llm-help-description":"Widget & macro reference — the building blocks for in-tiddler graphs","llm-help-sort":"04","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Widgets & Macros

The plugin ships these user-facing primitives. Mix them inside `<$graph>` to compose a graph.

* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/graph")` — `<$graph>` container
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/node")` — `<$node>` and `<$node.default>`
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/edge")` — `<$edge>` plus `$action.addedge`
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/edges-typed")` — `<$edges.typed>` (preset edges from fields/functions)
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-writable")` — `<$nodes.writable>` (drop/add/delete)
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/nodes-neighbors")` — `<$nodes.neighbors>` (incoming + outgoing)
* `llm_help("plugins/tw5-graph/widgets/properties")` — `<$properties>` and the `properties.*` family
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/readme":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/readme","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! External LLM Help

llm-help pages for third-party TiddlyWiki plugins. This plugin contributes a `plugins/<plugin-slug>/...` subtree to the [[llm-help|$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help]] browser so an LLM working in a tiddler can discover and read context-aware documentation for the wiki's installed plugins.

!! Why a separate plugin?

* Third-party plugins (`flibbles/graph`, `evidentlyCube/AutoComplete`, ...) cannot be modified to ship their own llm-help pages.
* Centralising their documentation here keeps it versioned, distributable, and discoverable through the same `llm_help` MCP tool the user already trusts.
* Each documented plugin gets its own subfolder under `pages/` so adding a new one is a self-contained change.

!! Layout

```
pages/
  plugins.tid                    -- root listing of documented plugins
  tw5-graph/
    root.tid                     -- llm-help-path: plugins/tw5-graph
    overview.tid                 -- plugins/tw5-graph/overview
    concepts.tid
    concepts/<thing>.tid
    widgets.tid
    widgets/<widget>.tid
    filters.tid
    templates.tid
    recipes.tid
    recipes/<task>.tid
    gotchas.tid
  <next-plugin>/
    ...
```

Every page is tagged `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` and gated by `llm-help-context: plugins/<plugin-slug>` so the entire subtree is opt-in.

!! For maintainers

Use the project skill ``/tw-derive-llmhelp-from-plugin <plugin-id>`` to generate or refresh a plugin's page tree. The skill reads TiddlyWiki gotcha memories first, analyses the plugin source, proposes a tree, and writes the pages.

!! Requirements

* [[$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help]] -- the help browser and `llm_help` tool.
"},"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/settings":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/external-llm-help/settings","created":"20260413120000000","modified":"20260413120000000","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! External LLM Help

This plugin has no configurable settings. It contributes documentation pages to the [[LLM Help|$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help]] browser under the `plugins/` path.

!! Documented plugins

<$list filter="[all[shadows+tiddlers]tag[$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page]get[llm-help-context]enlist-input[]prefix[plugins/]removeprefix[plugins/]] +[unique[]sort[]]" emptyMessage="No plugin documentation contributed yet.">
* `plugins/<$text text=<<currentTiddler>>/>`
</$list>

To browse them, open the [[LLM Help|$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help]] settings tab and navigate into the `plugins` topic. To enable visibility per plugin, toggle the corresponding context key (`plugins/<slug>`) in the context selector.
"}}}
